subject.title: Spanish B
subject.description:
**IB Spanish B** is a **Group 2: Language Acquisition** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **five prescribed themes: Identities, Experiences, Human ingenuity, Social organisation, and Sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive (listening and reading), productive (writing), and interactive (speaking) language skills while fostering intercultural understanding through exploration of Spanish-speaking cultures**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2020**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Writing task (choose 1 of 3 options) | 1h 15m | 1h 30m | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Reading and listening comprehension | 1h 45m | 2h | 50% |
| **Individual Oral (IA)** | Oral presentation and discussion | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 25% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear and appropriate purpose and focus throughout the essay.
The research question is clearly stated, sharply focused and consistently connected to each section of the discussion.
Transitions between contextual background, textual analysis and socio-historical implications are smooth and maintain alignment with the research focus.
weaknesses: Methodology is only described in passing and lacks a dedicated section outlining criteria for source selection and evaluation.
The introduction does not explicitly restate the research question to guide the reader from the outset.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the descriptors for the top band (5â6) in Focus and Method: the topic and research question are communicated with precision and sustained throughout the analysis. Although methodological rationale is implicit rather than explicitly detailed, the informed selection of sources and consistency of focus justify the maximum mark.

strengths: Source material is applied clearly and appropriately, with quotations and statistics integrated effectively to support arguments.
Subject knowledge of early 20th-century Spanish history and rhetorical theory is coherent and well structured.
Terminology and concepts (e.g., paralelismo, antÃ­tesis, patriarcado) are used accurately and consistently throughout the analysis.
weaknesses: Occasional generalisations in the historical context section would benefit from engagement with differing scholarly perspectives.
Some analytical points (e.g., on politeness strategies) lack reference to established theoretical frameworks.
Reliance on older statistical data could be strengthened by more recent corroborating evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay consistently demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge, effective use of subject-specific terminology and highly relevant application of primary and secondary sources, fulfilling the criteria for the highest band (5â6). The few generalisations and theoretical gaps do not undermine the overall excellence of Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Research draws on a wide range of primary and secondary sources clearly relevant to the research question.
Analysis intertwines close reading of rhetorical devices with socio-historical data to produce reasoned and generally well-supported interpretations.
Discussion weighs opposing views (e.g., Victoria Kentâs stance) and sustains a coherent argument from introduction to conclusion.
weaknesses: Critical appraisal of source reliability and bias (especially for non-academic sources) is only occasional.
Linkages between biographical details and rhetorical choices lack depth and could be more critically examined.
Alternative perspectives (e.g., Church influence, audience reception) are under-developed, limiting the sophistication of the evaluation.
The conclusion omits reflection on methodological limitations or potential biases.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The Critical Thinking performance sits firmly in the upper range of the good-to-very-good band (7â9). The work demonstrates logical argumentation, relevant research and consistent analysis, but stops short of the sustained, nuanced critique and depth of evaluation required for the topmost band (10â12). A score of 9 reflects its strong but not exceptional critical engagement.

strengths: Structure is clearly appropriate and complete, with logical headings, table of contents and appendices.
Layout elementsâincluding title page, word count, numbered pages, in-text citations and an APA bibliographyâare all present and largely correct.
The conclusion effectively revisits the research question and synthesizes the main findings in a coherent manner.
weaknesses: Minor APA inconsistencies (e.g., access dates, hanging indents) appear in the reference list.
Indentation and font styles in the bibliography are not uniform.
The page number for the appendices in the table of contents does not match the actual location.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets the criteria for a âclearly appropriate and completeâ structure and correct layout, despite minor formatting inconsistencies. These do not detract from readability or navigation, justifying the top mark of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: A single, sharply worded research question appears on the title page and is repeated in the introduction.  Its focus (the extent to which the speech mirrors prevailing male mind-sets) is narrow enough to be investigated in 4,000 words yet broad enough to allow discussion of rhetoric, history and politics.  Each section heading mirrors an element of the question, and analytical paragraphs consistently reference it (e.g., linking lexical choices to patriarchy).  Consequently the question remains a clear through-line that guides discussion and avoids digression.

Analytical sections intertwine close reading of rhetorical devices with historical data, producing reasoned interpretations of how language choices mirror patriarchal attitudes.  Counter-arguments (e.g., Victoria Kentâs stance) are addressed, and logical connections are usually sound.  Nonetheless, evaluation of alternative perspectives (such as the Churchâs role) is limited, preventing the analysis from achieving the consistently sophisticated insight required for the top band.

Quotations from the primary text are consistently integrated and analysed, and secondary works are used to corroborate factual statements about literacy rates, church influence and legal restrictions.  Citations appear whenever evidence is introduced, and examples are clearly tied to arguments, demonstrating a consistently relevant and appropriate application of source material.

Essential layout elementsâtitle page with word count, numbered pages, table of contents, in-text citations and an alphabetised APA bibliographyâare all included and largely accurate.  Small APA issues (e.g., inconsistent access dates) and occasional heading style mismatches remain, but overall layout meets the IB requirements and supports readability.

The student displays clear and coherent knowledge of early 20th-century Spanish socio-political history and of rhetorical theory.  Biographical information on Campoamor, the Second Republic, and church influence is accurately summarised and linked to the argument.  Sources are marshalled effectively to substantiate claims, indicating solid command of subject matter.

The monograph follows the required EE structure: abstract (omitted as per 2024 guide), introduction, body sections matching the table of contents, conclusion, bibliography and appendices.  Headings are logical and facilitate navigation.  Minor formatting inconsistencies (occasional underlining style differences) are present but do not detract from overall structural clarity, fulfilling the criteria for a âclearly appropriate and completeâ structure.

A wide range of primary and secondary sourcesâincluding parliamentary transcripts, academic monographs, historical databases and contemporary newspaper articlesâdemonstrates good breadth and clear relevance to the question.  However, evaluation of source reliability or bias is only occasional; for instance, the role of National Geographic or blog materials is not critically appraised.  The research is therefore âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ, fitting the 7-9 band.

The studentâs topicâthe degree to which Campoamorâs 1931 speech reflects contemporary Spanish attitudes toward womenâis introduced immediately, framed within its historical context and linked explicitly to Spanish B course content.  Throughout the essay the investigation stays firmly anchored to this focus, returning to the research question in each analytical subsection and again in the conclusion.  Transitions between contextual background, textual analysis, and wider socio-historical implications are smooth, demonstrating an accurate and purposeful communication of the topic and its relevance.

The discussion progresses logically from context to textual analysis to broader societal implications, leading to a clear, evidence-based conclusion.  The student weighs opposing views, notably Kentâs objections, and generally sustains a coherent argument.  Critical evaluation of methodological limitations and source bias is present but brief, so while the discussion is âgood and effectiveâ, it stops short of the sustained, nuanced critique characteristic of the 10-12 band.

The essay explains, in passing, that the primary source is the full speech and that secondary articles were chosen to supply historical context; however, rationale for selecting particular scholars or evaluating their reliability is largely implicit.  There is no dedicated methodology section outlining analytical procedures or criteria for source selection.  Nevertheless, the student does describe comparing rhetorical devices with historical realities and shows some informed selection of academic articles, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band rather than the limited 1-2 range.

Specialised rhetorical termsâparalelismo, antÃ­tesis, metÃ¡foraâas well as historical and political vocabulary (sufragio, Segunda RepÃºblica, patriarcado) are used accurately and consistently.  Definitions are provided where needed, and the terminology is applied correctly in the analysis, showing confident grasp of key concepts.

annotations.comment: The identification of parallelism and antithesis effectively ties rhetorical structure to persuasive impact, illustrating appropriate use of terminology.
The rhetorical analysis of pronoun usage (âSeÃ±ores diputadosâ) accurately connects language to power dynamics, demonstrating strong critical thinking.
Several web sources lack full retrieval dates or author information; verify and complete all entries to comply with APA standards.
Citation style varies between journal, web and book entries; ensure consistent use of italics, punctuation and indentation across all reference types.
References are not fully alphabetised across page breaks, which disrupts the intended order; reorder entries to maintain strict alphabetical sequence.
Inclusion of diverse source typesâbooks, journal articles and archival web documentsâdemonstrates effective and relevant application of source material.
Integration of primary quotations such as âSeÃ±ores diputadosâ demonstrates consistently relevant and appropriate use of source material in support of analysis.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically organized, facilitating easy navigation through the introductory, analytical and concluding sections.
Close reading of metaphorical references to Anatole France is well chosen and illuminates irony and intertextual strategy in Campoamorâs speech.
The research question is stated clearly on the title page, anchoring the investigation with a precise focus on Campoamorâs 1931 speech and its reflection of Spanish attitudes toward women.
The title, subject details and word count are correctly formatted at the start of the report, fulfilling IB requirements for presentation and formatting.

The âConsideraciÃ³n de la sociedad espaÃ±olaâ section integrates historical context well but occasionally generalises; deeper engagement with differing historical perspectives would strengthen the argument.
References to the Catholic Churchâs influence are minimal despite its historical importance; integrating evidence on church power could enrich the socio-historical analysis.
The use of statistical evidence on literacy trends provides a solid empirical basis for challenging patriarchal assumptions, effectively applying source material.
The conclusion concisely revisits the research question and synthesizes the main analytical findings, maintaining clear and appropriate focus.
Methodology is described only in passing; a dedicated section outlining criteria for selecting and evaluating both primary and secondary sources would strengthen the methodological rigor.
The introduction effectively applies secondary sources to frame the historical context of the Second Republic, demonstrating coherent subject knowledge and relevance to the research focus.
The biographical background on Campoamor is detailed but lacks critical linkage to how her personal experiences directly shaped her rhetorical choices in the 1931 speech.
Biographical details are accurately summarised and supported by credible secondary sources, reflecting clear and coherent subject knowledge.
The start of the analysis section clearly signals the shift to close reading of the speech, maintaining focus and method alignment throughout the essay.
Transitions between biographical subsections and analysis could be smoother; adding explicit linking sentences would enhance structural coherence.
The argument on politeness strategies lacks reference to rhetorical or politeness theory; citing a definition of politeness or ethos would clarify the analytical framework.
The page number listed for âApÃ©ndicesâ in the table of contents does not match the actual appendix location, which may confuse readers when locating supplementary material.
The introduction does not explicitly restate the research question to guide the reader; inserting a clear question statement here would reinforce the focus from the outset.
The bibliography is comprehensive and alphabetised, demonstrating thorough research across primary and secondary source types.
The analysis of direct address to male deputies illustrates effective use of primary text to reveal the speechâs reflection of patriarchal power structures.
The discussion of constitutional change would benefit from explicit engagement with counter-arguments or alternative political views to deepen critical evaluation.
Indentation and font styles in the final reference list are inconsistent, reducing readability; apply a uniform hanging indent and typeface.
Secondary sources such as blog entries or National Geographic are treated as equally authoritative; an evaluation of their reliability or bias would enhance research quality.
Heading levels (h2 vs. h3) are applied inconsistently, disrupting visual hierarchy; standardise heading styles for clearer presentation.
Analysis of repetition would benefit from exploring its emotional effect on the deputies; integrating audience reception theory could deepen insight.
APA bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent access date formats and occasionally omit DOI or publisher details; standardizing these elements will enhance credibility.
Linking rhetorical devices to the broader context of political instability demonstrates strong critical thinking and relevance to the research question.
Reliance on 1910 data would be strengthened by seeking more recent corroborating statistics to support claims about literacy trends leading up to 1931.
The conclusion omits discussion of methodological limitations or potential biases; reflecting on these aspects would demonstrate deeper critical evaluation.
The historical examples of womenâs protest movements are listed without critical analysis of their direct relevance to Spain in 1931; refine connections to the speechâs context.
